title: Single Element in Sorted Array
hints: Use binary search to find the single element in O(log N) time.
Check edge cases first: single element array, first element is unique, last element is unique.
For each mid position, check if it's the single element by comparing with neighbors.
If A[mid] != A[mid-1] and A[mid] != A[mid+1], it's the single element.
Use the pattern of pairs: before the single element, pairs are at even-odd indices. After the single element, pairs are at odd-even indices.
If mid is even and A[mid] == A[mid+1], the single element is on the right. Otherwise, it's on the left.
